LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS JACK EUGENE TEETERS, AS EXECUTOR OF THE THOMAS F. WHITE ESTATE AND SHANE WHITE, AS TRUSTEE OF THE THOMAS F. WHITE 1991 TRUST'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT- THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND TO THE WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY AND ANTICIPATORY BREACH CAUSES OF ACTION. THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION ACCRUED ON FEBRUARY 19, 2008, WHEN MR. WHITE TERMINATED PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF FILED THIS UNTIMELY ACTION ON DECEMBER 2, 2013. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 4, 5, 7, AND 8. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT MR. WHITE'S DUTY TO PAY AROSE ONCE HE WAS RELEASED FROM PRISON. THE COMPLAINT ALSO ALLEGES THAT MR. WHITE DIED WHILE INCARCERATED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT RECOVER BECAUSE THE CONDITION PRECEDENT DID NOT OCCUR. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL CAUSE OF ACTION. THAT THEORY DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE WAS BARGAINED FOR AND IS GOVERNED BY CONTRACTUAL PRINCIPLES. RES JUDICATA DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE (1) THE JUDGMENT IN CASE #494198 IS NOT FINAL AND (2) THE JUDGMENT WITH REGARD TO THE 2005 CONTRACT WAS NOT ON THE MERITS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF PATRICK KELLY'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER ON DEMURRER. THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OR VACATE IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS UNTIMELY BECAUSE THE COURT ENTERED JUDGMENT. SEE RAMON V. AEROSPACE CORP. (1996) 50 CAL.APP.4TH 1233, 1236; APRI INS. CO. V. SUP. CRT. (1999) 76 CAL.APP.4TH 176, 181-182. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE NEW LAW OR DECLARATION REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 1008. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A BASIS FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473. PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE RAISED THE REPLY ISSUE AT THE HEARING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Verified Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Patrick Kelly, Tel. 415.769.9524, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
Britt Evangelist, Esq. of Swanson & McNamara, Tel. 415.477.3800, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matte was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF PATRICK KELLY's Motion For Leave To File Verified Second Amended Complaint as follows: Plaintiffs Patrick Kelly's motion for leave to file a verified second amended complaint is denied. While amendments to complaints are liberally granted, Mr. Kelly's proposed second amended complaint adds a claim for lost wages on a cause of action that is time-barred. The Court of Appeal's decision, which is binding on this court, states that Mr. Kelly's "cause of action for breach of the 2003 employment contract alleged that the contract was breached when White wrongfully terminated appellant's employment on February 19, 2008. The present complaint not having been filed until December 2013, this cause of action was barred by the two-year statute of limitation." Moreover, adding a new substantive claim seeking a large sum of money less than two weeks before trial would be extremely prejudicial to defendant Shane Kelley.

Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JACK EUGENE TEETERS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The summary adjudication order in the prior case was not based on failure of a condition precedent. That order is entirely consistent with and are governed by the court of appeal's decision in this case which states, in footnote 12 on page 20, that "this cause of action did not accrue until White died and appellant's creditor's claim was rejected by the estate." Defendant's res judicata argument is thus without merit and none of plaintiff's claims are time barred as a matter of law. They all accrued when the creditor's claim was denied. Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion & Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - PATRICK KELLY, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. GEOFFREY ROTWEIN, TEL. NO. 1-415-397-0860 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRICK KELLY'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is denied. Pursuant to CCP 1008(a), a motion for reconsideration must be based on new facts or law and supported by declaration stating "what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." "To be entitled to reconsideration, a party should show that (1) evidence of new or different facts exist, and (2) the party has a satisfactory explanation for failing to produce such evidence at an earlier time." Kalivas v. Barry Controls Corp., (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1152, 1160-1161. "A party seeking reconsideration also must provide a satisfactory explanation for the failure to produce evidence at an earlier time." NY Times Co. v. Sup. Crt., (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212. Here, plaintiff's declaration does not comply with CCP 1008. The declaration is not under penalty of perjury under the laws of California per CCP 2015.5. The declaration does not state "what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." Most importantly, plaintiff's motion does not clearly describe what the new facts or evidence is. Plaintiff does not sufficiently explain why he could not appear at the prior hearing by telephone. Plaintiff was not diligent in discovering the difference between a promise and condition precedent. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

WHITE 1991 TRUST's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT- The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend to the wrongful termination in violation of public policy and anticipatory breach causes of action. Those causes of action accrued on February 19, 2008, when Mr. White terminated Plaintiff and Plaintiff filed this untimely action on December 2, 2013. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 2, 4, 5, 7, and 8. The complaint alleges that Mr. White's duty to pay arose once he was released from prison. The complaint also alleges that Mr. White died while incarcerated. Plaintiff cannot recover because the condition precedent did not occur. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the promissory estoppel cause of action. That theory does not apply because plaintiff's reliance was bargained for and is governed by contractual principles. Res judicata does not apply because (1) the judgment in case #494198 is not final and (2) the judgment with regard to the 2005 contract was not on the merits. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
